text.NRSV: We declare to you what was from the beginning, what we have heard, what we have seen with our eyes, what we have looked at and touched with our hands, concerning the word of life—
text.Rotherham: That which was from the beginning, which we have heard, which we have seen with our eyes, which we for ourselves gazed upon, and our hands did handle, concerning the Word of Life,—
text.KJV: That which was from the beginning, which we have heard, which we have seen with our eyes, which we have looked upon, and our hands have handled, of the Word of life;
text.NABRE: I. Prologue Chapter 1 The Word of Life What was from the beginning, what we have heard, what we have seen with our eyes, what we looked upon and touched with our hands concerns the Word of life—
text.DRB: That which was from the beginning, which we have heard, which we have seen with our eyes, which we have looked upon, and our hands have handled, of the word of life:

text.NRSV: this life was revealed, and we have seen it and testify to it, and declare to you the eternal life that was with the Father and was revealed to us—
text.Rotherham: And, the Life, was made manifest, and we have seen, and are bearing witness, and announcing unto you, the Age-abiding Life, which, indeed, was with the Father, and was made manifest unto us;
text.KJV: (For the life was manifested, and we have seen it, and bear witness, and shew unto you that eternal life, which was with the Father, and was manifested unto us;)
text.NABRE: for the life was made visible; we have seen it and testify to it and proclaim to you the eternal life that was with the Father and was made visible to us—
text.DRB: (and the life was manifested: and we have seen, and do testify, and declare unto you the life eternal which was with the Father, and hath appeared to us)

text.NRSV: we declare to you what we have seen and heard so that you also may have fellowship with us; and truly our fellowship is with the Father and with his Son Jesus Christ.
text.Rotherham: That which we have seen and heard, are we announcing, even unto you, in order that, ye too, may have fellowship with us, and, our own fellowship also, may be with the Father, and with his Son Jesus Christ.
text.KJV: That which we have seen and heard declare we unto you, that ye also may have fellowship with us: and truly our fellowship is with the Father, and with his Son Jesus Christ.
text.NABRE: what we have seen and heard we proclaim now to you, so that you too may have fellowship with us; for our fellowship is with the Father and with his Son, Jesus Christ.
text.DRB: that which we have seen and have heard, we declare unto you, that you also may have society with us, and our society may be with the Father and with his Son Jesus Christ.

text.NRSV: We are writing these things so that our joy may be complete.
text.Rotherham: And, these things, are we writing, in order that, our joy, may be made full.
text.KJV: And these things write we unto you, that your joy may be full.
text.NABRE: We are writing this so that our joy may be complete.
text.DRB: And these things we write to you, that you may rejoice, and your joy may be full.

text.NRSV: This is the message we have heard from him and proclaim to you, that God is light and in him there is no darkness at all.
text.Rotherham: And this is the message which we have heard from him, and are reporting unto you,—that, God, is, light, and in him is, no darkness at all.
text.KJV: This then is the message which we have heard of him, and declare unto you, that God is light, and in him is no darkness at all.
text.NABRE: II. God As Light God Is Light. Now this is the message that we have heard from him and proclaim to you: God is light, and in him there is no darkness at all.
text.DRB: And this is the annunciation which we have heard of him, and declare unto you, That God is light, and in him there is no darkness:

text.NRSV: If we say that we have fellowship with him while we are walking in darkness, we lie and do not do what is true;
text.Rotherham: If we say—We have, fellowship, with him! and, in darkness, are walking, we are dealing falsely, and not doing the truth;
text.KJV: If we say that we have fellowship with him, and walk in darkness, we lie, and do not the truth:
text.NABRE: If we say, “We have fellowship with him,” while we continue to walk in darkness, we lie and do not act in truth.
text.DRB: If we shall say that we have society with him, and walk in darkness, we lie, and do not the truth.

text.NRSV: but if we walk in the light as he himself is in the light, we have fellowship with one another, and the blood of Jesus his Son cleanses us from all sin.
text.Rotherham: Whereas, if, in the light, we are walking, as, he, is in the light, we have, fellowship one with another, and, the blood of Jesus his Son, is cleansing us from all sin.
text.KJV: But if we walk in the light, as he is in the light, we have fellowship one with another, and the blood of Jesus Christ his Son cleanseth us from all sin.
text.NABRE: But if we walk in the light as he is in the light, then we have fellowship with one another, and the blood of his Son Jesus cleanses us from all sin.
text.DRB: But if we walk in the light, as he also is in the light: we have society one toward another, and the blood of Jesus Christ his Son cleanseth us from all sin.

text.NRSV: If we say that we have no sin, we deceive ourselves, and the truth is not in us.
text.Rotherham: If we say—Sin, have we none! we are deceiving, ourselves, and, the truth, is not in us.
text.KJV: If we say that we have no sin, we deceive ourselves, and the truth is not in us.
text.NABRE: If we say, “We are without sin,” we deceive ourselves, and the truth is not in us.
text.DRB: If we shall say that we have no sin, we seduce ourselves, and the truth is not in us.

text.NRSV: If we confess our sins, he who is faithful and just will forgive us our sins and cleanse us from all unrighteousness.
text.Rotherham: If we are confessing our sins, faithful, is he and, righteous—that he should forgive us our sins, and cleanse us from all unrighteousness.
text.KJV: If we confess our sins, he is faithful and just to forgive us our sins, and to cleanse us from all unrighteousness.
text.NABRE: If we acknowledge our sins, he is faithful and just and will forgive our sins and cleanse us from every wrongdoing.
text.DRB: If we confess our sins, he is faithful & just, for to forgive us our sins, and to cleanse us from all iniquity.

text.NRSV: If we say that we have not sinned, we make him a liar, and his word is not in us.
text.Rotherham: If we say—We have not sinned! false, are we making, him, and, his word, is not in us.
text.KJV: If we say that we have not sinned, we make him a liar, and his word is not in us.
text.NABRE: If we say, “We have not sinned,” we make him a liar, and his word is not in us.
text.DRB: If we shall say that we have not sinned, we make him a liar, and his word is not in us.

text.NRSV: My little children, I am writing these things to you so that you may not sin. But if anyone does sin, we have an advocate with the Father, Jesus Christ the righteous;
text.Rotherham: My dear children! these things, am I writing unto you, in order that ye may not be committing sin. And, if anyone should commit sin, an Advocate, have we, with the Father, Jesus Christ, the Righteous;
text.KJV: My little children, these things write I unto you, that ye sin not. And if any man sin, we have an advocate with the Father, Jesus Christ the righteous:
text.NABRE: Chapter 2 Christ and His Commandments. My children, I am writing this to you so that you may not commit sin. But if anyone does sin, we have an Advocate with the Father, Jesus Christ the righteous one.
text.DRB: My little children, these things I write to you, that you sin not. But and if any man shall sin, we have an Advocate with the Father, Jesus Christ the just:

text.NRSV: and he is the atoning sacrifice for our sins, and not for ours only but also for the sins of the whole world.
text.Rotherham: And, he, is, a propitiation, concerning our sins,—and, not concerning our own only, but, also concerning those of the whole world.
text.KJV: And he is the propitiation for our sins: and not for ours only, but also for the sins of the whole world.
text.NABRE: He is expiation for our sins, and not for our sins only but for those of the whole world.
text.DRB: and he is the propitiation for our sins: and not for ours only, but also for the whole world's.

text.NRSV: Now by this we may be sure that we know him, if we obey his commandments.
text.Rotherham: And, hereby, perceive we, that we understand him,—if, his commandments, we are keeping.
text.KJV: And hereby we do know that we know him, if we keep his commandments.
text.NABRE: The way we may be sure that we know him is to keep his commandments.
text.DRB: And in this we know that we have known him, if we observe his commandments.

text.NRSV: Whoever says, “I have come to know him,” but does not obey his commandments, is a liar, and in such a person the truth does not exist;
text.Rotherham: He that saith—I understand him! And, his commandments, is not keeping, is false, and, in him, the truth is not!
text.KJV: He that saith, I know him, and keepeth not his commandments, is a liar, and the truth is not in him.
text.NABRE: Whoever says, “I know him,” but does not keep his commandments is a liar, and the truth is not in him.
text.DRB: He that saith he knoweth him, and keepeth not his commandments, is a liar, and the truth is not in him:

text.NRSV: but whoever obeys his word, truly in this person the love of God has reached perfection. By this we may be sure that we are in him:
text.Rotherham: But whosoever may be keeping his word, of a truth, in this man, the love of God hath been made perfect. Hereby, perceive we, that, in him, we are.
text.KJV: But whoso keepeth his word, in him verily is the love of God perfected: hereby know we that we are in him.
text.NABRE: But whoever keeps his word, the love of God is truly perfected in him. This is the way we may know that we are in union with him:
text.DRB: But he that keepeth his word, in him in very deed the charity of God is perfited: in this we know that we be in him.

text.NRSV: whoever says, “I abide in him,” ought to walk just as he walked.
text.Rotherham: He that saith, that, in him, he abideth, ought, just as He walked, himself also, to be walking.
text.KJV: He that saith he abideth in him ought himself also so to walk, even as he walked.
text.NABRE: whoever claims to abide in him ought to live [just] as he lived. The New Commandment.
text.DRB: He that saith he abideth in him, ought even as he walked, himself also to walk.

text.NRSV: Beloved, I am writing you no new commandment, but an old commandment that you have had from the beginning; the old commandment is the word that you have heard.
text.Rotherham: Beloved! no new commandment, am I writing unto you; but an old commandment, which ye have been holding from the beginning: The old commandment is the word which ye have heard.
text.KJV: Brethren, I write no new commandment unto you, but an old commandment which ye had from the beginning. The old commandment is the word which ye have heard from the beginning.
text.NABRE: Beloved, I am writing no new commandment to you but an old commandment that you had from the beginning. The old commandment is the word that you have heard.
text.DRB: My dearest, I write not a new commandment to you, but an old commandment which you had from the beginning. The old commandment is the word which you have heard.

text.NRSV: Yet I am writing you a new commandment that is true in him and in you, because the darkness is passing away and the true light is already shining.
text.Rotherham: Again, a new commandment, am I writing unto you,—which thing is true, in him and in you, because, the darkness, is passing away, and, the real light, already is shining.
text.KJV: Again, a new commandment I write unto you, which thing is true in him and in you: because the darkness is past, and the true light now shineth.
text.NABRE: And yet I do write a new commandment to you, which holds true in him and among you, for the darkness is passing away, and the true light is already shining.
text.DRB: Again a new commandment write I to you, which thing is true both in him and in you: because the darkness is passed, and the true light now shineth.

text.NRSV: Whoever says, “I am in the light,” while hating a brother or sister, is still in the darkness.
text.Rotherham: He that saith he is, in the light, and hateth, his brother, is, in the darkness, until even now!
text.KJV: He that saith he is in the light, and hateth his brother, is in darkness even until now.
text.NABRE: Whoever says he is in the light, yet hates his brother, is still in the darkness.
text.DRB: He that saith he is in the light, and hateth his brother, is in the darkness even until now.

text.NRSV: Whoever loves a brother or sister lives in the light, and in such a person there is no cause for stumbling.
text.Rotherham: He that loveth his brother, is abiding, in the light, and, cause of stumbling, in him, is there none.
text.KJV: He that loveth his brother abideth in the light, and there is none occasion of stumbling in him.
text.NABRE: Whoever loves his brother remains in the light, and there is nothing in him to cause a fall.
text.DRB: He that loveth his brother, abideth in the light, and scandal is not in him.

text.NRSV: But whoever hates a brother or sister is in the darkness, walks in the darkness, and does not know the way to go, because the darkness has brought on blindness.
text.Rotherham: Whereas, he that hateth his brother, in the darkness, dwelleth, and in the darkness, walketh; and knoweth not whither he is drifting, because the, darkness, hath blinded his eyes.
text.KJV: But he that hateth his brother is in darkness, and walketh in darkness, and knoweth not whither he goeth, because that darkness hath blinded his eyes.
text.NABRE: Whoever hates his brother is in darkness; he walks in darkness and does not know where he is going because the darkness has blinded his eyes. Members of the Community.
text.DRB: But he that hateth his brother, is in the darkness, and walketh in the darkness, and knoweth not whither he goeth, because the darkness hath blinded his eyes.

text.NRSV: I am writing to you, little children, because your sins are forgiven on account of his name.
text.Rotherham: I write unto you, dear children, because your sins have been forgiven you, for the sake of his name:
text.KJV: I write unto you, little children, because your sins are forgiven you for his name’s sake.
text.NABRE: I am writing to you, children, because your sins have been forgiven for his name’s sake.
text.DRB: I write unto you little children, because your sins are forgiven you for his name.

text.NRSV: I am writing to you, fathers, because you know him who is from the beginning. I am writing to you, young people, because you have conquered the evil one.
text.Rotherham: I write unto you, fathers, because ye understand him who was from the beginning: I write unto you, young men, because ye have overcome the wicked one. I have written unto you, little children, because ye understand the Father:
text.KJV: I write unto you, fathers, because ye have known him that is from the beginning. I write unto you, young men, because ye have overcome the wicked one. I write unto you, little children, because ye have known the Father.
text.NABRE: I am writing to you, fathers, because you know him who is from the beginning. I am writing to you, young men, because you have conquered the evil one.
text.DRB: I write unto you fathers, because you have known him which is from the beginning. I write unto you young men, because you have overcome the wicked one.

text.NRSV: I write to you, children, because you know the Father. I write to you, fathers, because you know him who is from the beginning. I write to you, young people, because you are strong and the word of God abides in you, and you have conquered the evil one.
text.Rotherham: I have written unto you, fathers, because ye understand him who was from the beginning: I have written you, young men, because ye are, strong, and the word of God, within you, abideth, and ye have overcome the wicked one.
text.KJV: I have written unto you, fathers, because ye have known him that is from the beginning. I have written unto you, young men, because ye are strong, and the word of God abideth in you, and ye have overcome the wicked one.
text.NABRE: I write to you, children, because you know the Father. I write to you, fathers, because you know him who is from the beginning. I write to you, young men, because you are strong and the word of God remains in you, and you have conquered the evil one.
text.DRB: I write to you infants, because you have known the Father. I write unto you young men, because you are strong, and the word of God abideth in you, and you have overcome the wicked one.

text.NRSV: Do not love the world or the things in the world. The love of the Father is not in those who love the world;
text.Rotherham: Be not loving the world, nor yet the things that are in the world: if anyone be loving the world, the love of the Father is not in him.
text.KJV: Love not the world, neither the things that are in the world. If any man love the world, the love of the Father is not in him.
text.NABRE: Do not love the world or the things of the world. If anyone loves the world, the love of the Father is not in him.
text.DRB: Love not the world, nor those things which are in the world. If any man love the world, the charity of the Father is not in him.

text.NRSV: for all that is in the world—the desire of the flesh, the desire of the eyes, the pride in riches—comes not from the Father but from the world.
text.Rotherham: Because, all that is in the world—the coveting of the flesh, the coveting of the eyes, and the vain grandeur of life—is not of the Father, but is, of the world;
text.KJV: For all that is in the world, the lust of the flesh, and the lust of the eyes, and the pride of life, is not of the Father, but is of the world.
text.NABRE: For all that is in the world, sensual lust, enticement for the eyes, and a pretentious life, is not from the Father but is from the world.
text.DRB: because all that is in the world, is the concupiscence of the flesh, and the concupiscence of the eyes, and the pride of life, which is not of the Father, but is of the world.

text.NRSV: And the world and its desire are passing away, but those who do the will of God live forever.
text.Rotherham: And, the world, passeth away, and the coveting [thereof], but, he that doeth the will of God, endureth unto times age-abiding.
text.KJV: And the world passeth away, and the lust thereof: but he that doeth the will of God abideth for ever.
text.NABRE: Yet the world and its enticement are passing away. But whoever does the will of God remains forever.
text.DRB: And the world passeth and the concupiscence thereof. But he that doeth the will of God, abideth for ever.

text.NRSV: Children, it is the last hour! As you have heard that antichrist is coming, so now many antichrists have come. From this we know that it is the last hour.
text.Rotherham: Little children! it is, the last hour; and, just as ye have heard that, an antichrist, is coming, even now, antichrists have become, many, whence we perceive that it is, the last hour:
text.KJV: Little children, it is the last time: and as ye have heard that antichrist shall come, even now are there many antichrists; whereby we know that it is the last time.
text.NABRE: Antichrists. Children, it is the last hour; and just as you heard that the antichrist was coming, so now many antichrists have appeared. Thus we know this is the last hour.
text.DRB: Little children, it is the last hour, & as you have heard, that Antichrist cometh: now there are become many Antichrists, whereby we know, that it is the last hour.

text.NRSV: They went out from us, but they did not belong to us; for if they had belonged to us, they would have remained with us. But by going out they made it plain that none of them belongs to us.
text.Rotherham: From among us, they went out, but they were not of us; for, if, of us, they had been, they would in that case have abode with us; but [it came to pass] in order that they might be made manifest, because, all, are not of us.
text.KJV: They went out from us, but they were not of us; for if they had been of us, they would no doubt have continued with us: but they went out, that they might be made manifest that they were not all of us.
text.NABRE: They went out from us, but they were not really of our number; if they had been, they would have remained with us. Their desertion shows that none of them was of our number.
text.DRB: They went out from us; but they were not of us. For if they had been of us, they would surely have remained with us: but that they may be manifest that they are not all of us.

text.NRSV: But you have been anointed by the Holy One, and all of you have knowledge.
text.Rotherham: And, ye, have, an anointing, from the Holy One,—Ye all, know:
text.KJV: But ye have an unction from the Holy One, and ye know all things.
text.NABRE: But you have the anointing that comes from the holy one, and you all have knowledge.
text.DRB: But you have the unction from the Holy one, and know all things.

text.NRSV: I write to you, not because you do not know the truth, but because you know it, and you know that no lie comes from the truth.
text.Rotherham: I have not written unto you because ye know not the truth, but because ye know it, and because, no falsehood, is, of the truth.
text.KJV: I have not written unto you because ye know not the truth, but because ye know it, and that no lie is of the truth.
text.NABRE: I write to you not because you do not know the truth but because you do, and because every lie is alien to the truth.
text.DRB: I have not written to you as to them that know not the truth, but as to them that know it: and that no lie is of the truth.

text.NRSV: Who is the liar but the one who denies that Jesus is the Christ? This is the antichrist, the one who denies the Father and the Son.
text.Rotherham: Who, is the False One;—save he that denieth that, Jesus, is the Christ? The same, is the Antichrist,—he that denieth the Father and the Son.
text.KJV: Who is a liar but he that denieth that Jesus is the Christ? He is antichrist, that denieth the Father and the Son.
text.NABRE: Who is the liar? Whoever denies that Jesus is the Christ. Whoever denies the Father and the Son, this is the antichrist.
text.DRB: Who is a liar, but he which denieth that Jesus is Christ? This is Antichrist which denieth the Father and the Son.

text.NRSV: No one who denies the Son has the Father; everyone who confesses the Son has the Father also.
text.Rotherham: Whosoever denieth the Son, neither hath he, the Father: He that confesseth the Son, hath, the Father also.
text.KJV: Whosoever denieth the Son, the same hath not the Father: [but] he that acknowledgeth the Son hath the Father also.
text.NABRE: No one who denies the Son has the Father, but whoever confesses the Son has the Father as well.
text.DRB: Every one that denieth the Son, neither hath he the Father. He that confesseth the Son, hath the Father also.

text.NRSV: Let what you heard from the beginning abide in you. If what you heard from the beginning abides in you, then you will abide in the Son and in the Father.
text.Rotherham: As for you, what ye have heard from the beginning, in you, let it abide. If, in you, shall abide, that which, from the beginning, ye have heard, ye also, in the Son, and [in] the Father, shall abide.
text.KJV: Let that therefore abide in you, which ye have heard from the beginning. If that which ye have heard from the beginning shall remain in you, ye also shall continue in the Son, and in the Father.
text.NABRE: Life from God’s Anointing. Let what you heard from the beginning remain in you. If what you heard from the beginning remains in you, then you will remain in the Son and in the Father.
text.DRB: You, that which you have heard from the beginning, let it abide in you. If that abide in you which you have heard from the beginning, you also shall abide in the Son & the Father.

text.NRSV: And this is what he has promised us, eternal life.
text.Rotherham: And, this, is the promise, which he hath promised unto us,—The age-abiding life.
text.KJV: And this is the promise that he hath promised us, even eternal life.
text.NABRE: And this is the promise that he made us: eternal life.
text.DRB: And this is the promise which he promised us, life everlasting.

text.NRSV: I write these things to you concerning those who would deceive you.
text.Rotherham: These things, have I written unto you, concerning them who would lead you astray.
text.KJV: These things have I written unto you concerning them that seduce you.
text.NABRE: I write you these things about those who would deceive you.
text.DRB: These things have I written to you concerning them that seduce you.

text.NRSV: As for you, the anointing that you received from him abides in you, and so you do not need anyone to teach you. But as his anointing teaches you about all things, and is true and is not a lie, and just as it has taught you, abide in him.
text.Rotherham: And, as for you, the anointing which ye have received from him, abideth in you, and ye have, no need, that anyone be teaching you; but, as, his anointing, is teaching you, and is, true, and is no falsehood, even just as it hath taught you, abide ye in him.
text.KJV: But the anointing which ye have received of him abideth in you, and ye need not that any man teach you: but as the same anointing teacheth you of all things, and is truth, and is no lie, and even as it hath taught you, ye shall abide in him.
text.NABRE: As for you, the anointing that you received from him remains in you, so that you do not need anyone to teach you. But his anointing teaches you about everything and is true and not false; just as it taught you, remain in him.
text.DRB: And you, the unction which you have received from him, let it abide in you. And you have no need that any man teach you: but as his unction teacheth you of all things, and it is true, and it is no lie. And as it hath taught you, abide in him.

text.NRSV: And now, little children, abide in him, so that when he is revealed we may have confidence and not be put to shame before him at his coming.
text.Rotherham: And, now, dear children, abide ye in him, in order that, if he be made manifest, we may have boldness, and not be shamed away from him by his presence.
text.KJV: And now, little children, abide in him; that, when he shall appear, we may have confidence, and not be ashamed before him at his coming.
text.NABRE: Children of God. And now, children, remain in him, so that when he appears we may have confidence and not be put to shame by him at his coming.
text.DRB: And now little children abide in him: that when he shall appear, we may have confidence, and not be confounded of him in his coming.

text.NRSV: If you know that he is righteous, you may be sure that everyone who does right has been born of him.
text.Rotherham: If ye know that he is, righteous, ye perceive that—whosoever doeth righteousness, of him, hath been born.
text.KJV: If ye know that he is righteous, ye know that every one that doeth righteousness is born of him.
text.NABRE: If you consider that he is righteous, you also know that everyone who acts in righteousness is begotten by him.
text.DRB: If you know that he is just, know ye that every one also which doeth justice, is born of him.

text.NRSV: See what love the Father has given us, that we should be called children of God; and that is what we are. The reason the world does not know us is that it did not know him.
text.Rotherham: Behold, what manner of love, the Father hath bestowed upon us—that, children of God, we should be called,—and such we are! Therefore, the world understandeth us not, because it understood not him.
text.KJV: Behold, what manner of love the Father hath bestowed upon us, that we should be called the sons of God: therefore the world knoweth us not, because it knew him not.
text.NABRE: Chapter 3 See what love the Father has bestowed on us that we may be called the children of God. Yet so we are. The reason the world does not know us is that it did not know him.
text.DRB: See what manner of charity the Father hath given us, that we should be named and be the sons of God. For this cause the world doth not know us, because it hath not known him.

text.NRSV: Beloved, we are God’s children now; what we will be has not yet been revealed. What we do know is this: when he is revealed, we will be like him, for we will see him as he is.
text.Rotherham: Beloved! now, are we, children of God; and, not yet, hath it been made manifest, what we shall be,—We know that, if it should be made manifest, like unto him, shall we be, because we shall see him, just as he is.
text.KJV: Beloved, now are we the sons of God, and it doth not yet appear what we shall be: but we know that, when he shall appear, we shall be like him; for we shall see him as he is.
text.NABRE: Beloved, we are God’s children now; what we shall be has not yet been revealed. We do know that when it is revealed we shall be like him, for we shall see him as he is.
text.DRB: My dearest, now we are the sons of God; & it hath not yet appeared what we shall be. We know that when he shall appear, we shall be like to him: because we shall see him as he is.

text.NRSV: And all who have this hope in him purify themselves, just as he is pure.
text.Rotherham: And, whosoever hath this hope on him, is purifying himself, just as, He, is pure.
text.KJV: And every man that hath this hope in him purifieth himself, even as he is pure.
text.NABRE: Everyone who has this hope based on him makes himself pure, as he is pure.
text.DRB: And every one that hath this hope in him, sanctifieth himself, as he also is holy.

text.NRSV: Everyone who commits sin is guilty of lawlessness; sin is lawlessness.
text.Rotherham: Whosoever is committing sin, lawlessness also, is committing, and, sin, is, lawlessness;
text.KJV: Whosoever committeth sin transgresseth also the law: for sin is the transgression of the law.
text.NABRE: Avoiding Sin. Everyone who commits sin commits lawlessness, for sin is lawlessness.
text.DRB: Every one that committeth sin, committeth also iniquity: and sin is iniquity.

text.NRSV: You know that he was revealed to take away sins, and in him there is no sin.
text.Rotherham: And ye know, that, He, was made manifest—in order that, our sins, he should take away, and, sin, in him, is there none.
text.KJV: And ye know that he was manifested to take away our sins; and in him is no sin.
text.NABRE: You know that he was revealed to take away sins, and in him there is no sin.
text.DRB: And you know that he appeared to take away our sins: and sin in him there is none.

text.NRSV: No one who abides in him sins; no one who sins has either seen him or known him.
text.Rotherham: Whosoever, in him, doth abide, is not sinning: whosoever is sinning, hath not seen him, and doth not understand him.
text.KJV: Whosoever abideth in him sinneth not: whosoever sinneth hath not seen him, neither known him.
text.NABRE: No one who remains in him sins; no one who sins has seen him or known him.
text.DRB: Every one that abideth in him, sinneth not: and every one that sinneth, hath not seen him, nor known him.

text.NRSV: Little children, let no one deceive you. Everyone who does righteousness is righteous, just as he is righteous.
text.Rotherham: Dear children! Let, no one, lead you astray! He that is doing righteousness, is, righteous, just as, He, is righteous:
text.KJV: Little children, let no man deceive you: he that doeth righteousness is righteous, even as he is righteous.
text.NABRE: Children, let no one deceive you. The person who acts in righteousness is righteous, just as he is righteous.
text.DRB: Little children, let no man seduce you. He that doeth justice, is just: even as he also is just.

text.NRSV: Everyone who commits sin is of the devil, for the devil has been sinning from the beginning. The Son of God was revealed for this purpose, to destroy the works of the devil.
text.Rotherham: He that is committing sin, is, of the adversary, because, from the beginning, the adversary is sinning. To this end, was the Son of God made manifest, in order that he might undo the works of the adversary.
text.KJV: He that committeth sin is of the devil; for the devil sinneth from the beginning. For this purpose the Son of God was manifested, that he might destroy the works of the devil.
text.NABRE: Whoever sins belongs to the devil, because the devil has sinned from the beginning. Indeed, the Son of God was revealed to destroy the works of the devil.
text.DRB: He that committeth sin, is of the devil: because the devil sinneth from the beginning. For this, appeared the Son of God, that he might dissolve the works of the devil.

text.NRSV: Those who have been born of God do not sin, because God’s seed abides in them; they cannot sin, because they have been born of God.
text.Rotherham: Whosoever hath been born of God, is not committing, sin, because, a seed of him, within him, abideth; and he cannot be committing sin, because, of God, hath he been born.
text.KJV: Whosoever is born of God doth not commit sin; for his seed remaineth in him: and he cannot sin, because he is born of God.
text.NABRE: No one who is begotten by God commits sin, because God’s seed remains in him; he cannot sin because he is begotten by God.
text.DRB: Every one that is born of God, committeth not sin: because his seed abideth in him, and he cannot sin because he is born of God.

text.NRSV: The children of God and the children of the devil are revealed in this way: all who do not do what is right are not from God, nor are those who do not love their brothers and sisters.
text.Rotherham: Herein, are, manifest, the children of God, and the children of the adversary: Whoever is not doing righteousness, is not of God, nor yet he that is not loving his brother.
text.KJV: In this the children of God are manifest, and the children of the devil: whosoever doeth not righteousness is not of God, neither he that loveth not his brother.
text.NABRE: In this way, the children of God and the children of the devil are made plain; no one who fails to act in righteousness belongs to God, nor anyone who does not love his brother.
text.DRB: In this are the children of God manifest, and the children of the devil. Every one that is not just, is not of God, and he that loveth not his brother.

text.NRSV: For this is the message you have heard from the beginning, that we should love one another.
text.Rotherham: Because, this, is the message which ye have heard from the beginning—that we should be loving one another,—
text.KJV: For this is the message that ye heard from the beginning, that we should love one another.
text.NABRE: III. Love for One Another For this is the message you have heard from the beginning: we should love one another,
text.DRB: Because this is the annunciation, which you have heard from the beginning, That you love one another.

text.NRSV: We must not be like Cain who was from the evil one and murdered his brother. And why did he murder him? Because his own deeds were evil and his brother’s righteous.
text.Rotherham: Not just as, Cain, was, of the wicked one, and slew his brother! And, for what cause, slew he him? Because, his works, were, wicked, whereas, those of his brother, were, righteous.
text.KJV: Not as Cain, who was of that wicked one, and slew his brother. And wherefore slew he him? Because his own works were evil, and his brother’s righteous.
text.NABRE: unlike Cain who belonged to the evil one and slaughtered his brother. Why did he slaughter him? Because his own works were evil, and those of his brother righteous.
text.DRB: Not as Cain, who was of the wicked, and killed his brother. And for what cause killed he him? Because his works were wicked: but his brother's, just.

text.NRSV: Do not be astonished, brothers and sisters, if the world hates you.
text.Rotherham: Be not marveling, brethren, if, the world, is hating you:
text.KJV: Marvel not, my brethren, if the world hate you.
text.NABRE: Do not be amazed, [then,] brothers, if the world hates you.
text.DRB: Marvel not, brethren, if the world hate you.

text.NRSV: We know that we have passed from death to life because we love one another. Whoever does not love abides in death.
text.Rotherham: We, know, that we have passed over out of death into life, because we love the brethren: he that loveth not, abideth in death.
text.KJV: We know that we have passed from death unto life, because we love the brethren. He that loveth not his brother abideth in death.
text.NABRE: We know that we have passed from death to life because we love our brothers. Whoever does not love remains in death.
text.DRB: We know that we are translated from death to life, because we love the brethren. He that loveth not, abideth in death.

text.NRSV: All who hate a brother or sister are murderers, and you know that murderers do not have eternal life abiding in them.
text.Rotherham: Whosoever is hating his brother, is, a murderer; and ye know that, no murderer, hath life age-during, within him abiding.
text.KJV: Whosoever hateth his brother is a murderer: and ye know that no murderer hath eternal life abiding in him.
text.NABRE: Everyone who hates his brother is a murderer, and you know that no murderer has eternal life remaining in him.
text.DRB: Whosoever hateth his brother, is a murderer. And you know that no murderer hath life everlasting abiding in himself.

text.NRSV: We know love by this, that he laid down his life for us—and we ought to lay down our lives for one another.
text.Rotherham: Hereby, have we come to understand love: in that, He, for us, his life laid down; and, we, ought, for the brethren, our lives to lay down.
text.KJV: Hereby perceive we the love of God, because he laid down his life for us: and we ought to lay down our lives for the brethren.
text.NABRE: The way we came to know love was that he laid down his life for us; so we ought to lay down our lives for our brothers.
text.DRB: In this we have known the charity of God, because he hath yielded his life for us: and we ought to yield our lives for the brethren.

text.NRSV: How does God’s love abide in anyone who has the world’s goods and sees a brother or sister in need and yet refuses help?
text.Rotherham: But, whoso hath this world’s goods, and beholdeth his brother having, need, and shutteth up his tender affections from him, how, is, the love of God, abiding in him?
text.KJV: But whoso hath this world’s good, and seeth his brother have need, and shutteth up his bowels of compassion from him, how dwelleth the love of God in him?
text.NABRE: If someone who has worldly means sees a brother in need and refuses him compassion, how can the love of God remain in him?
text.DRB: He that shall have the substance of the world, and shall see his brother have need, and shall shut his bowels from him: how doth the charity of God abide in him?

text.NRSV: Little children, let us love, not in word or speech, but in truth and action.
text.Rotherham: Dear children! Let us not be loving in word, nor yet with the tongue, but in deed and truth.
text.KJV: My little children, let us not love in word, neither in tongue; but in deed and in truth.
text.NABRE: Children, let us love not in word or speech but in deed and truth. Confidence Before God.
text.DRB: My little children, let us not love in word, nor in tongue, but in deed and truth.

text.NRSV: And by this we will know that we are from the truth and will reassure our hearts before him
text.Rotherham: Hereby, shall we get to know, that, of the truth, we are, and, before him, shall persuade our heart;
text.KJV: And hereby we know that we are of the truth, and shall assure our hearts before him.
text.NABRE: [Now] this is how we shall know that we belong to the truth and reassure our hearts before him
text.DRB: In this we know that we are of the truth: and in his sight we shall persuade our hearts.

text.NRSV: whenever our hearts condemn us; for God is greater than our hearts, and he knows everything.
text.Rotherham: Because, if our own heart condemn us, greater, is God, than our heart, and perceiveth all things.
text.KJV: For if our heart condemn us, God is greater than our heart, and knoweth all things.
text.NABRE: in whatever our hearts condemn, for God is greater than our hearts and knows everything.
text.DRB: For if our heart do reprehend us, God is greater than our heart, and knoweth all things.

text.NRSV: Beloved, if our hearts do not condemn us, we have boldness before God;
text.Rotherham: Beloved! if our heart be not condemning us, boldness, have we, towards God;
text.KJV: Beloved, if our heart condemn us not, then have we confidence toward God.
text.NABRE: Beloved, if [our] hearts do not condemn us, we have confidence in God
text.DRB: My dearest, if our heart do not reprehend us, we have confidence toward God.

text.NRSV: and we receive from him whatever we ask, because we obey his commandments and do what pleases him.
text.Rotherham: And, whatsoever we are asking, we are receiving from him, because, his commandments, are we keeping, and, the things that are pleasing before him, are we doing.
text.KJV: And whatsoever we ask, we receive of him, because we keep his commandments, and do those things that are pleasing in his sight.
text.NABRE: and receive from him whatever we ask, because we keep his commandments and do what pleases him.
text.DRB: And whatsoever we shall ask, we shall receive of him: because we keep his commandments, and do those things which are pleasing before him.

text.NRSV: And this is his commandment, that we should believe in the name of his Son Jesus Christ and love one another, just as he has commanded us.
text.Rotherham: And, this, is his commandment,—that we should believe in the name of his Son Jesus Christ, and be loving one another—just as he gave a commandment unto us.
text.KJV: And this is his commandment, That we should believe on the name of his Son Jesus Christ, and love one another, as he gave us commandment.
text.NABRE: And his commandment is this: we should believe in the name of his Son, Jesus Christ, and love one another just as he commanded us.
text.DRB: And this is his commandment, that we believe in the name of his Son Jesus Christ: and love one another, as he hath given commandment unto us.

text.NRSV: All who obey his commandments abide in him, and he abides in them. And by this we know that he abides in us, by the Spirit that he has given us.
text.Rotherham: And, he that keepeth his commandments, in him, abideth, and, he, in him. And, hereby, perceive we, that he abideth in us, by reason of the Spirit which, unto us, he hath given.
text.KJV: And he that keepeth his commandments dwelleth in him, and he in him. And hereby we know that he abideth in us, by the Spirit which he hath given us.
text.NABRE: Those who keep his commandments remain in him, and he in them, and the way we know that he remains in us is from the Spirit that he gave us.
text.DRB: And he that keepeth his commandments, abideth in him, and he in him. And in this we know that he abideth in us, by the Spirit which he hath given us.

text.NRSV: Beloved, do not believe every spirit, but test the spirits to see whether they are from God; for many false prophets have gone out into the world.
text.Rotherham: Beloved! not in every spirit, believe ye, but test the spirits, whether they are, of God; because, many false prophets, have gone out into the world.
text.KJV: Beloved, believe not every spirit, but try the spirits whether they are of God: because many false prophets are gone out into the world.
text.NABRE: Chapter 4 Testing the Spirits. Beloved, do not trust every spirit but test the spirits to see whether they belong to God, because many false prophets have gone out into the world.
text.DRB: MY dearest, believe not every spirit, but prove the spirits if they be of God: because, many false prophets are gone out into the world.

text.NRSV: By this you know the Spirit of God: every spirit that confesses that Jesus Christ has come in the flesh is from God,
text.Rotherham: Hereby, do ye perceive the Spirit of God:—every spirit that confesseth Jesus Christ as having come, in flesh, is, of God;
text.KJV: Hereby know ye the Spirit of God: Every spirit that confesseth that Jesus Christ is come in the flesh is of God:
text.NABRE: This is how you can know the Spirit of God: every spirit that acknowledges Jesus Christ come in the flesh belongs to God,
text.DRB: In this is the spirit of God known. Every spirit that confesseth Jesus Christ to have come in flesh, is of God:

text.NRSV: and every spirit that does not confess Jesus is not from God. And this is the spirit of the antichrist, of which you have heard that it is coming; and now it is already in the world.
text.Rotherham: And, every spirit that doth not confess Jesus, of God, is not. And, this, is the [spirit] of the Antichrist, touching which ye have heard that it cometh: even now, is it, in the world, already.
text.KJV: And every spirit that confesseth not that Jesus Christ is come in the flesh is not of God: and this is that spirit of antichrist, whereof ye have heard that it should come; and even now already is it in the world.
text.NABRE: and every spirit that does not acknowledge Jesus does not belong to God. This is the spirit of the antichrist that, as you heard, is to come, but in fact is already in the world.
text.DRB: and every spirit that dissolveth Jesus, is not of God: and this is Antichrist, of whom you have heard that he cometh, and now he is in the world.

text.NRSV: Little children, you are from God, and have conquered them; for the one who is in you is greater than the one who is in the world.
text.Rotherham: Ye, are, of God, dear children, and have overcome them; because, greater, is he that is in you, than he that is in the world.
text.KJV: Ye are of God, little children, and have overcome them: because greater is he that is in you, than he that is in the world.
text.NABRE: You belong to God, children, and you have conquered them, for the one who is in you is greater than the one who is in the world.
text.DRB: You are of God, little children, and have overcome him. Because greater is he that is in you, than he that is in the world.

text.NRSV: They are from the world; therefore what they say is from the world, and the world listens to them.
text.Rotherham: They, are, of the world: For this cause, of the world, they speak, and the world, unto them, doth hearken.
text.KJV: They are of the world: therefore speak they of the world, and the world heareth them.
text.NABRE: They belong to the world; accordingly, their teaching belongs to the world, and the world listens to them.
text.DRB: They are of the world: therefore of the world they speak, and the world heareth them.

text.NRSV: We are from God. Whoever knows God listens to us, and whoever is not from God does not listen to us. From this we know the spirit of truth and the spirit of error.
text.Rotherham: We, are, of God: he that is getting to understand God, hearkeneth unto us,—whoso is not of God, hearkeneth not unto us: from this, perceive we—the spirit of truth, and the spirit of error.
text.KJV: We are of God: he that knoweth God heareth us; he that is not of God heareth not us. Hereby know we the spirit of truth, and the spirit of error.
text.NABRE: We belong to God, and anyone who knows God listens to us, while anyone who does not belong to God refuses to hear us. This is how we know the spirit of truth and the spirit of deceit.
text.DRB: We are of God. He that knoweth God, heareth us. He that is not of God, heareth us not. In this we know the spirit of truth, and the spirit of error.

text.NRSV: Beloved, let us love one another, because love is from God; everyone who loves is born of God and knows God.
text.Rotherham: Beloved! let us be loving one another; because, love, is, of God, and, whosoever loveth, of God, hath been born, and is getting to understand God:
text.KJV: Beloved, let us love one another: for love is of God; and every one that loveth is born of God, and knoweth God.
text.NABRE: God’s Love and Christian Life. Beloved, let us love one another, because love is of God; everyone who loves is begotten by God and knows God.
text.DRB: My dearest, let us love one another: because charity is of God. And every one that loveth, is born of God, & knoweth God.

text.NRSV: Whoever does not love does not know God, for God is love.
text.Rotherham: He that doth not love, doth not understand God, because, God, is, love.
text.KJV: He that loveth not knoweth not God; for God is love.
text.NABRE: Whoever is without love does not know God, for God is love.
text.DRB: He that loveth not, knoweth not God: because God is charity.

text.NRSV: God’s love was revealed among us in this way: God sent his only Son into the world so that we might live through him.
text.Rotherham: Herein, hath the love of God in us, been made manifest, that, his only-begotten Son, God sent into the world, in order that we might live through him.
text.KJV: In this was manifested the love of God toward us, because that God sent his only begotten Son into the world, that we might live through him.
text.NABRE: In this way the love of God was revealed to us: God sent his only Son into the world so that we might have life through him.
text.DRB: In this hath the charity of God appeared in us, because God hath sent his only-begotten Son into the world, that we may live by him.

text.NRSV: In this is love, not that we loved God but that he loved us and sent his Son to be the atoning sacrifice for our sins.
text.Rotherham: Herein, is love: not that, we, have loved God, but that, he, loved us, and sent forth his Son, as a propitiation concerning our sins.
text.KJV: Herein is love, not that we loved God, but that he loved us, and sent his Son to be the propitiation for our sins.
text.NABRE: In this is love: not that we have loved God, but that he loved us and sent his Son as expiation for our sins.
text.DRB: In this is charity: not as though we have loved him, but because he hath loved us, and sent his Son a propitiation for our sins.

text.NRSV: Beloved, since God loved us so much, we also ought to love one another.
text.Rotherham: Beloved! If, in this way, God, loved us, we also, ought to love, one another.
text.KJV: Beloved, if God so loved us, we ought also to love one another.
text.NABRE: Beloved, if God so loved us, we also must love one another.
text.DRB: My dearest, if God hath so loved us, we also ought to love one another.

text.NRSV: No one has ever seen God; if we love one another, God lives in us, and his love is perfected in us.
text.Rotherham: Upon God, hath no one, at any time, gazed: If we love one another, God, in us, abideth, and, his love, hath been perfected within us.
text.KJV: No man hath seen God at any time. If we love one another, God dwelleth in us, and his love is perfected in us.
text.NABRE: No one has ever seen God. Yet, if we love one another, God remains in us, and his love is brought to perfection in us.
text.DRB: God no man hath seen at any time. If we love one another, God abideth in us, and his charity in us is perfited.

text.NRSV: By this we know that we abide in him and he in us, because he has given us of his Spirit.
text.Rotherham: Hereby, perceive we—that, in him, are we abiding, and, he, in us,—in that, of his Spirit, hath he given unto us.
text.KJV: Hereby know we that we dwell in him, and he in us, because he hath given us of his Spirit.
text.NABRE: This is how we know that we remain in him and he in us, that he has given us of his Spirit.
text.DRB: In this we know that we abide in him, and he in us: because he of his Spirit hath given to us.

text.NRSV: And we have seen and do testify that the Father has sent his Son as the Savior of the world.
text.Rotherham: And, we, for ourselves have gazed, and are bearing witness—that, the Father, sent forth the Son, as Saviour of the world.
text.KJV: And we have seen and do testify that the Father sent the Son to be the Saviour of the world.
text.NABRE: Moreover, we have seen and testify that the Father sent his Son as savior of the world.
text.DRB: And we have seen, and do testify, that the Father hath sent his Son the Saviour of the world.

text.NRSV: God abides in those who confess that Jesus is the Son of God, and they abide in God.
text.Rotherham: Whosoever shall confess, that, Jesus [Christ] is the Son of God, God, in him, abideth, and, he, in God.
text.KJV: Whosoever shall confess that Jesus is the Son of God, God dwelleth in him, and he in God.
text.NABRE: Whoever acknowledges that Jesus is the Son of God, God remains in him and he in God.
text.DRB: Whosoever shall confess that Jesus is the Son of God, God abideth in him, and he in God.

text.NRSV: So we have known and believe the love that God has for us. God is love, and those who abide in love abide in God, and God abides in them.
text.Rotherham: And, we, have come to understand and to trust the love which, God, hath, in us. God, is, love; and, he that abideth in love, in God, abideth, and, God, in him [abideth].
text.KJV: And we have known and believed the love that God hath to us. God is love; and he that dwelleth in love dwelleth in God, and God in him.
text.NABRE: We have come to know and to believe in the love God has for us. God is love, and whoever remains in love remains in God and God in him.
text.DRB: And we have known and have believed the charity, which God hath in us. God is charity: and he that abideth in charity, abideth in God, and God in him.

text.NRSV: Love has been perfected among us in this: that we may have boldness on the day of judgment, because as he is, so are we in this world.
text.Rotherham: Herein, hath love with us, been made perfect,—in order that, boldness, we might have in the day of judging,—in that, just as, He, is, we also, are, in this world.
text.KJV: Herein is our love made perfect, that we may have boldness in the day of judgment: because as he is, so are we in this world.
text.NABRE: In this is love brought to perfection among us, that we have confidence on the day of judgment because as he is, so are we in this world.
text.DRB: In this is charity perfited with us, that we may have confidence in the day of judgement: because as he is, we also are in the world.

text.NRSV: There is no fear in love, but perfect love casts out fear; for fear has to do with punishment, and whoever fears has not been perfected in love.
text.Rotherham: Fear, existeth not, in love, but, perfect love, casteth fear, outside; because, fear, hath correction: he that feareth, hath not been made perfect, in love.
text.KJV: There is no fear in love; but perfect love casteth out fear: because fear hath torment. He that feareth is not made perfect in love.
text.NABRE: There is no fear in love, but perfect love drives out fear because fear has to do with punishment, and so one who fears is not yet perfect in love.
text.DRB: Fear is not in charity: but perfect charity casteth out fear, because fear hath painfulness. And he that feareth, is not perfect in charity.

text.NRSV: We love because he first loved us.
text.Rotherham: We, love, because, he, first loved us:—
text.KJV: We love him, because he first loved us.
text.NABRE: We love because he first loved us.
text.DRB: Let us therefore love God, because God first hath loved us.

text.NRSV: Those who say, “I love God,” and hate their brothers or sisters, are liars; for those who do not love a brother or sister whom they have seen, cannot love God whom they have not seen.
text.Rotherham: If one should say, I love God, and should be hating, his brother, false, is he; for, he that doth not love his brother, whom he hath seen, God, whom he hath not seen, he cannot love!
text.KJV: If a man say, I love God, and hateth his brother, he is a liar: for he that loveth not his brother whom he hath seen, how can he love God whom he hath not seen?
text.NABRE: If anyone says, “I love God,” but hates his brother, he is a liar; for whoever does not love a brother whom he has seen cannot love God whom he has not seen.
text.DRB: If any man shall say, that I love God; and hateth his brother, he is a liar. For he that loveth not his brother whom he seeth, God whom he seeth not, how can he love?

text.NRSV: The commandment we have from him is this: those who love God must love their brothers and sisters also.
text.Rotherham: And, this commandment, have we from him,—that, he who loveth God, love, his brother also.
text.KJV: And this commandment have we from him, That he who loveth God love his brother also.
text.NABRE: This is the commandment we have from him: whoever loves God must also love his brother.
text.DRB: And this commandment we have from God: that he which loveth God, love also his brother.

text.NRSV: Everyone who believes that Jesus is the Christ has been born of God, and everyone who loves the parent loves the child.
text.Rotherham: Whosoever believeth that, Jesus, is the Christ, of God, hath been born: and, whosoever loveth him that begat, loveth him that hath been begotten of him.
text.KJV: Whosoever believeth that Jesus is the Christ is born of God: and every one that loveth him that begat loveth him also that is begotten of him.
text.NABRE: Chapter 5 Faith Is Victory over the World. Everyone who believes that Jesus is the Christ is begotten by God, and everyone who loves the father loves [also] the one begotten by him.
text.DRB: Whosoever believeth that Jesus is Christ, is born of God. And every one that loveth him which begat, loveth him also which was born of him.

text.NRSV: By this we know that we love the children of God, when we love God and obey his commandments.
text.Rotherham: Hereby, perceive we, that we love the children of God, as soon as, God, we love, and, his commandments, we are doing.
text.KJV: By this we know that we love the children of God, when we love God, and keep his commandments.
text.NABRE: In this way we know that we love the children of God when we love God and obey his commandments.
text.DRB: In this we know that we love the children of God: when as we love God, and keep his commandments.

text.NRSV: For the love of God is this, that we obey his commandments. And his commandments are not burdensome,
text.Rotherham: For, this is the love of God—that, his commandments, we be keeping, and, his commandments, are not burdensome;
text.KJV: For this is the love of God, that we keep his commandments: and his commandments are not grievous.
text.NABRE: For the love of God is this, that we keep his commandments. And his commandments are not burdensome,
text.DRB: For this is the charity of God, that we keep his commandments: and his commandments are not heavy.

text.NRSV: for whatever is born of God conquers the world. And this is the victory that conquers the world, our faith.
text.Rotherham: Because, whatsoever hath been born of God, overcometh the world; and, this, is the victory that hath overcome the world—our faith.
text.KJV: For whatsoever is born of God overcometh the world: and this is the victory that overcometh the world, even our faith.
text.NABRE: for whoever is begotten by God conquers the world. And the victory that conquers the world is our faith.
text.DRB: Because all that is born of God, overcometh the world: And this is the victory which overcometh the world, our faith.

text.NRSV: Who is it that conquers the world but the one who believes that Jesus is the Son of God?
text.Rotherham: [And] who is he that overcometh the world, save he that believeth that, Jesus, is the Son of God?
text.KJV: Who is he that overcometh the world, but he that believeth that Jesus is the Son of God?
text.NABRE: Who [indeed] is the victor over the world but the one who believes that Jesus is the Son of God?
text.DRB: Who is he that overcometh the world, but he that believeth that Jesus is the Son of God?

text.NRSV: This is the one who came by water and blood, Jesus Christ, not with the water only but with the water and the blood. And the Spirit is the one that testifies, for the Spirit is the truth.
text.Rotherham: This, is he that came through means of water and blood, Jesus Christ: not, by the water, only, but, by the water and by the blood,—and, the Spirit, it is, that is bearing witness, because, the Spirit, is the truth.
text.KJV: This is he that came by water and blood, even Jesus Christ; not by water only, but by water and blood. And it is the Spirit that beareth witness, because the Spirit is truth.
text.NABRE: This is the one who came through water and blood, Jesus Christ, not by water alone, but by water and blood. The Spirit is the one that testifies, and the Spirit is truth.
text.DRB: This is he that came by water & blood Jesus Christ: not in water only, but in water and blood. And it is the Spirit which testifieth, that Christ is the truth.

text.NRSV: There are three that testify:
text.Rotherham: Because, three, are they who are bearing witness—
text.KJV: For there are three that bear record in heaven, the Father, the Word, and the Holy Ghost: and these three are one.
text.NABRE: So there are three that testify,
text.DRB: For there be three which give testimony in heaven, the Father, the Word, and the Holy Ghost. And these three be one.

text.NRSV: the Spirit and the water and the blood, and these three agree.
text.Rotherham: The Spirit, and the Water, and the Blood; and, the three, are, [witnesses], unto one thing.
text.KJV: And there are three that bear witness in earth, the spirit, and the water, and the blood: and these three agree in one.
text.NABRE: the Spirit, the water, and the blood, and the three are of one accord.
text.DRB: And there be three which give testimony in earth: the spirit, water, and blood: and these three be one.

text.NRSV: If we receive human testimony, the testimony of God is greater; for this is the testimony of God that he has testified to his Son.
text.Rotherham: If, the witness of men, we receive, the witness of God, is, greater. Because, this, is the witness of God—in that he hath borne witness concerning his Son,—
text.KJV: If we receive the witness of men, the witness of God is greater: for this is the witness of God which he hath testified of his Son.
text.NABRE: If we accept human testimony, the testimony of God is surely greater. Now the testimony of God is this, that he has testified on behalf of his Son.
text.DRB: If we receive the testimony of men, the testimony of God is greater. Because this is the testimony of God which is greater, that he hath testified of his Son.

text.NRSV: Those who believe in the Son of God have the testimony in their hearts. Those who do not believe in God have made him a liar by not believing in the testimony that God has given concerning his Son.
text.Rotherham: He that believeth on the Son of God, hath the witness, within himself: He that doth not believe God, false, hath made him,—because he hath not believed on the witness which, God, hath witnessed, concerning his Son—
text.KJV: He that believeth on the Son of God hath the witness in himself: he that believeth not God hath made him a liar; because he believeth not the record that God gave of his Son.
text.NABRE: Whoever believes in the Son of God has this testimony within himself. Whoever does not believe God has made him a liar by not believing the testimony God has given about his Son.
text.DRB: He that believeth in the Son of God, hath the testimony of God in himself. He that believeth not the Son, maketh him a liar: because he believeth not in the testimony which God hath testified of his Son.

text.NRSV: And this is the testimony: God gave us eternal life, and this life is in his Son.
text.Rotherham: And, this, is the witness:—that, life age-abiding, hath God given unto us, and, this life, is, in his Son:
text.KJV: And this is the record, that God hath given to us eternal life, and this life is in his Son.
text.NABRE: And this is the testimony: God gave us eternal life, and this life is in his Son.
text.DRB: And this is the testimony, that God hath given us life everlasting. And this life is in his Son.

text.NRSV: Whoever has the Son has life; whoever does not have the Son of God does not have life.
text.Rotherham: He that hath the Son, hath, the life,—he that hath not the Son of God, hath not, the life.
text.KJV: He that hath the Son hath life; and he that hath not the Son of God hath not life.
text.NABRE: Whoever possesses the Son has life; whoever does not possess the Son of God does not have life.
text.DRB: He that hath the Son, hath life. He that hath not the Son of God, hath not life.

text.NRSV: I write these things to you who believe in the name of the Son of God, so that you may know that you have eternal life.
text.Rotherham: These things, have I written unto you—in order that ye may know that ye have, Life Age-abiding—unto you who believe on the name of the Son of God.
text.KJV: These things have I written unto you that believe on the name of the Son of God; that ye may know that ye have eternal life, and that ye may believe on the name of the Son of God.
text.NABRE: IV. Epilogue Prayer for Sinners. I write these things to you so that you may know that you have eternal life, you who believe in the name of the Son of God.
text.DRB: These things I write to you, that you may know that you have eternal life which believe in the name of the Son of God.

text.NRSV: And this is the boldness we have in him, that if we ask anything according to his will, he hears us.
text.Rotherham: And, this, is the boldness which we have towards him: that, if, anything, we ask, according to his will, He doth hearken unto us.
text.KJV: And this is the confidence that we have in him, that, if we ask any thing according to his will, he heareth us:
text.NABRE: And we have this confidence in him, that if we ask anything according to his will, he hears us.
text.DRB: And this is the confidence which we have toward him: that, whatsoever we shall ask according to his will, he heareth us.

text.NRSV: And if we know that he hears us in whatever we ask, we know that we have obtained the requests we have made of him.
text.Rotherham: And, if we know that he doth hearken unto us, whatsoever we ask, we know that we have the things asked, which we have asked of him,
text.KJV: And if we know that he hear us, whatsoever we ask, we know that we have the petitions that we desired of him.
text.NABRE: And if we know that he hears us in regard to whatever we ask, we know that what we have asked him for is ours.
text.DRB: And we know that he heareth us whatsoever we shall ask: we know that we have the petitions which we request of him.

text.NRSV: If you see your brother or sister committing what is not a mortal sin, you will ask, and God will give life to such a one—to those whose sin is not mortal. There is sin that is mortal; I do not say that you should pray about that.
text.Rotherham: If one should see his brother committing a sin, not unto death, he shall ask, and He will grant unto him life,—for them who are sinning, not unto death. There, is, a sin, unto death: not concerning that, am I saying, that he should make request.
text.KJV: If any man see his brother sin a sin which is not unto death, he shall ask, and he shall give him life for them that sin not unto death. There is a sin unto death: I do not say that he shall pray for it.
text.NABRE: If anyone sees his brother sinning, if the sin is not deadly, he should pray to God and he will give him life. This is only for those whose sin is not deadly. There is such a thing as deadly sin, about which I do not say that you should pray.
text.DRB: He that knoweth his brother to sin a sin not to death, let him ask, and life shall be given him, sinning not to death. There is a sin to death: for that I say not that any man ask.

text.NRSV: All wrongdoing is sin, but there is sin that is not mortal.
text.Rotherham: All unrighteousness, is, sin, and there, is, a sin, not unto death.
text.KJV: All unrighteousness is sin: and there is a sin not unto death.
text.NABRE: All wrongdoing is sin, but there is sin that is not deadly.
text.DRB: All iniquity, is sin. And there is a sin to death.

text.NRSV: We know that those who are born of God do not sin, but the one who was born of God protects them, and the evil one does not touch them.
text.Rotherham: We know that, whosoever hath been born of God, is not committing sin,—Nay, he that hath been born of God, He keepeth him, and, the wicked one, doth not touch him.
text.KJV: We know that whosoever is born of God sinneth not; but he that is begotten of God keepeth himself, and that wicked one toucheth him not.
text.NABRE: We know that no one begotten by God sins; but the one begotten by God he protects, and the evil one cannot touch him.
text.DRB: We know that every one which is born of God, sinneth not: but the generation of God preserveth him, and the wicked one toucheth him not.

text.NRSV: We know that we are from God, and the whole world lies under the power of the evil one.
text.Rotherham: We know that, of God, are we; and, the whole world, in the wicked one, is lying.
text.KJV: And we know that we are of God, and the whole world lieth in wickedness.
text.NABRE: We know that we belong to God, and the whole world is under the power of the evil one.
text.DRB: We know that we are of God, and the whole world is set in wickedness.

text.NRSV: And we know that the Son of God has come and has given us understanding so that we may know him who is true; and we are in him who is true, in his Son Jesus Christ. He is the true God and eternal life.
text.Rotherham: We know, moreover, that, the Son of God, hath come, and hath given us insight, so that we are getting to understand, him that is Real,—and we are in him that is Real, in his Son Jesus Christ. This, is the Real God, and life age-abiding.
text.KJV: And we know that the Son of God is come, and hath given us an understanding, that we may know him that is true, and we are in him that is true, even in his Son Jesus Christ. This is the true God, and eternal life.
text.NABRE: We also know that the Son of God has come and has given us discernment to know the one who is true. And we are in the one who is true, in his Son Jesus Christ. He is the true God and eternal life.
text.DRB: And we know that the Son of God cometh: and he hath given us understanding, that we may know the true God, & may be in his true Son, This is the true God, & life everlasting.

text.NRSV: Little children, keep yourselves from idols.
text.Rotherham: Dear children! Guard yourselves from idols.
text.KJV: Little children, keep yourselves from idols. Amen.
text.NABRE: Children, be on your guard against idols.
text.DRB: My little children, keep yourselves from idols . Amen.
